members.name: AddBone
members.summary: Adds a new bone with the given name. Returns the new bone's index, or [code]-1[/code] if this method fails. [b]Note:[/b] Bone names should be unique, non empty, and cannot include the [code]:[/code] and [code]/[/code] characters.

members.name: Advance
members.summary: Manually advance the child [SkeletonModifier3D]s by the specified time (in seconds). [b]Note:[/b] The [param delta] is temporarily accumulated in the [Skeleton3D], and the deferred process uses the accumulated value to process the modification.

members.name: ClearBones
members.summary: Clear all the bones in this skeleton.

members.name: ClearBonesGlobalPoseOverride
members.summary: Removes the global pose override on all bones in the skeleton.

members.name: FindBone
members.summary: Returns the bone index that matches [param name] as its name. Returns [code]-1[/code] if no bone with this name exists.

members.name: ForceUpdateAllBoneTransforms
members.summary: Force updates the bone transforms/poses for all bones in the skeleton.

members.name: ForceUpdateBoneChildTransform
members.summary: Force updates the bone transform for the bone at [param bone_idx] and all of its children.

members.name: GetBoneChildren
members.summary: Returns an array containing the bone indexes of all the child node of the passed in bone, [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneCount
members.summary: Returns the number of bones in the skeleton.

members.name: GetBoneGlobalPose
members.summary: Returns the overall transform of the specified bone, with respect to the skeleton. Being relative to the skeleton frame, this is not the actual "global" transform of the bone. [b]Note:[/b] This is the global pose you set to the skeleton in the process, the final global pose can get overridden by modifiers in the deferred process, if you want to access the final global pose, use [signal SkeletonModifier3D.modification_processed].

members.name: GetBoneGlobalPoseNoOverride
members.summary: Returns the overall transform of the specified bone, with respect to the skeleton, but without any global pose overrides. Being relative to the skeleton frame, this is not the actual "global" transform of the bone.

members.name: GetBoneGlobalPoseOverride
members.summary: Returns the global pose override transform for [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneGlobalRest
members.summary: Returns the global rest transform for [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneMeta
members.signature: public Variant GetBoneMeta(int boneIdx, StringName key)
members.summary: Returns the metadata with the given [param key] for the bone at index [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneMetaList
members.summary: Returns the list of all metadata keys for the bone at index [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneName
members.summary: Returns the name of the bone at index [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneParent
members.summary: Returns the bone index which is the parent of the bone at [param bone_idx]. If -1, then bone has no parent. [b]Note:[/b] The parent bone returned will always be less than [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBonePose
members.summary: Returns the pose transform of the specified bone. [b]Note:[/b] This is the pose you set to the skeleton in the process, the final pose can get overridden by modifiers in the deferred process, if you want to access the final pose, use [signal SkeletonModifier3D.modification_processed].

members.name: GetBonePosePosition
members.summary: Returns the pose position of the bone at [param bone_idx]. The returned [Vector3] is in the local coordinate space of the [Skeleton3D] node.

members.name: GetBonePoseRotation
members.summary: Returns the pose rotation of the bone at [param bone_idx]. The returned [Quaternion] is local to the bone with respect to the rotation of any parent bones.

members.name: GetBonePoseScale
members.summary: Returns the pose scale of the bone at [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetBoneRest
members.summary: Returns the rest transform for a bone [param bone_idx].

members.name: GetConcatenatedBoneNames
members.summary: Returns all bone names concatenated with commas ([code],[/code]) as a single [StringName]. It is useful to set it as a hint for the enum property.

members.name: GetParentlessBones
members.summary: Returns an array with all of the bones that are parentless. Another way to look at this is that it returns the indexes of all the bones that are not dependent or modified by other bones in the Skeleton.

members.name: GetVersion
members.summary: Returns the number of times the bone hierarchy has changed within this skeleton, including renames. The Skeleton version is not serialized: only use within a single instance of Skeleton3D. Use for invalidating caches in IK solvers and other nodes which process bones.

members.name: HasBoneMeta
members.signature: public bool HasBoneMeta(int boneIdx, StringName key)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the bone at index [param bone_idx] has metadata with the given [param key].

members.name: IsBoneEnabled
members.summary: Returns whether the bone pose for the bone at [param bone_idx] is enabled.

members.name: LocalizeRests
members.summary: Returns all bones in the skeleton to their rest poses.

members.name: PhysicalBonesAddCollisionException
members.summary: Adds a collision exception to the physical bone. Works just like the [RigidBody3D] node.

members.name: PhysicalBonesRemoveCollisionException
members.summary: Removes a collision exception to the physical bone. Works just like the [RigidBody3D] node.

members.name: PhysicalBonesStartSimulation
members.summary: Tells the [PhysicalBone3D] nodes in the Skeleton to start simulating and reacting to the physics world. Optionally, a list of bone names can be passed-in, allowing only the passed-in bones to be simulated.

members.name: PhysicalBonesStopSimulation
members.summary: Tells the [PhysicalBone3D] nodes in the Skeleton to stop simulating.

members.name: RegisterSkin
members.summary: Binds the given Skin to the Skeleton.

members.name: ResetBonePose
members.summary: Sets the bone pose to rest for [param bone_idx].

members.name: ResetBonePoses
members.summary: Sets all bone poses to rests.

members.name: SetBoneEnabled
members.signature: public void SetBoneEnabled(int boneIdx, bool enabled)
members.summary: Disables the pose for the bone at [param bone_idx] if [code]false[/code], enables the bone pose if [code]true[/code].

members.name: SetBoneGlobalPose
members.signature: public void SetBoneGlobalPose(int boneIdx, Transform3D pose)
members.summary: Sets the global pose transform, [param pose], for the bone at [param bone_idx]. [b]Note:[/b] If other bone poses have been changed, this method executes a dirty poses recalculation and will cause performance to deteriorate. If you know that multiple global poses will be applied, consider using [method set_bone_pose] with precalculation.

members.name: SetBoneGlobalPoseOverride
members.signature: public void SetBoneGlobalPoseOverride(int boneIdx, Transform3D pose, float amount, bool persistent)
members.summary: Sets the global pose transform, [param pose], for the bone at [param bone_idx]. [param amount] is the interpolation strength that will be used when applying the pose, and [param persistent] determines if the applied pose will remain. [b]Note:[/b] The pose transform needs to be a global pose! To convert a world transform from a [Node3D] to a global bone pose, multiply the [method Transform3D.affine_inverse] of the node's [member Node3D.global_transform] by the desired world transform.

members.name: SetBoneMeta
members.signature: public void SetBoneMeta(int boneIdx, StringName key, Variant value)
members.summary: Sets the metadata with the given [param key] to [param value] for the bone at index [param bone_idx].

members.name: SetBoneName
members.signature: public void SetBoneName(int boneIdx, String name)
members.summary: Sets the bone name, [param name], for the bone at [param bone_idx].

members.name: SetBoneParent
members.signature: public void SetBoneParent(int boneIdx, int parentIdx)
members.summary: Sets the bone index [param parent_idx] as the parent of the bone at [param bone_idx]. If -1, then bone has no parent. [b]Note:[/b] [param parent_idx] must be less than [param bone_idx].

members.name: SetBonePose
members.signature: public void SetBonePose(int boneIdx, Transform3D pose)
members.summary: Sets the pose transform, [param pose], for the bone at [param bone_idx].

members.name: SetBonePosePosition
members.signature: public void SetBonePosePosition(int boneIdx, Vector3 position)
members.summary: Sets the pose position of the bone at [param bone_idx] to [param position]. [param position] is a [Vector3] describing a position local to the [Skeleton3D] node.

members.name: SetBonePoseRotation
members.signature: public void SetBonePoseRotation(int boneIdx, Quaternion rotation)
members.summary: Sets the pose rotation of the bone at [param bone_idx] to [param rotation]. [param rotation] is a [Quaternion] describing a rotation in the bone's local coordinate space with respect to the rotation of any parent bones.

members.name: SetBonePoseScale
members.signature: public void SetBonePoseScale(int boneIdx, Vector3 scale)
members.summary: Sets the pose scale of the bone at [param bone_idx] to [param scale].

members.name: SetBoneRest
members.signature: public void SetBoneRest(int boneIdx, Transform3D rest)
members.summary: Sets the rest transform for bone [param bone_idx].

members.name: UnparentBoneAndRest
members.summary: Unparents the bone at [param bone_idx] and sets its rest position to that of its parent prior to being reset.

members.name: AnimatePhysicalBones
members.summary: If you follow the recommended workflow and explicitly have [PhysicalBoneSimulator3D] as a child of [Skeleton3D], you can control whether it is affected by raycasting without running [method physical_bones_start_simulation], by its [member SkeletonModifier3D.active]. However, for old (deprecated) configurations, [Skeleton3D] has an internal virtual [PhysicalBoneSimulator3D] for compatibility. This property controls the internal virtual [PhysicalBoneSimulator3D]'s [member SkeletonModifier3D.active].

members.name: ModifierCallbackModeProcess
members.summary: Sets the processing timing for the Modifier.

members.name: MotionScale
members.summary: Multiplies the 3D position track animation. [b]Note:[/b] Unless this value is [code]1.0[/code], the key value in animation will not match the actual position value.

members.name: ShowRestOnly
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], forces the bones in their default rest pose, regardless of their values. In the editor, this also prevents the bones from being edited.

signals.name: BoneEnabledChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the bone at [param bone_idx] is toggled with [method set_bone_enabled]. Use [method is_bone_enabled] to check the new value.

signals.name: BoneListChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the list of bones changes, such as when calling [method add_bone], [method set_bone_parent], [method unparent_bone_and_rest], or [method clear_bones].

signals.name: PoseUpdated
signals.summary: Emitted when the pose is updated. [b]Note:[/b] During the update process, this signal is not fired, so modification by [SkeletonModifier3D] is not detected.

signals.name: RestUpdated
signals.summary: Emitted when the rest is updated.

signals.name: ShowRestOnlyChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the value of [member show_rest_only] changes.

signals.name: SkeletonUpdated
signals.summary: Emitted when the final pose has been calculated will be applied to the skin in the update process. This means that all [SkeletonModifier3D] processing is complete. In order to detect the completion of the processing of each [SkeletonModifier3D], use [signal SkeletonModifier3D.modification_processed].

values.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_IDLE
values.summary: Set a flag to process modification during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).

values.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_MANUAL
values.summary: Do not process modification. Use [method advance] to process the modification manually.

values.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_PHYSICS
values.summary: Set a flag to process modification during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).

constants.name: NOTIFICATION_UPDATE_SKELETON
constants.summary: Notification received when this skeleton's pose needs to be updated. In that case, this is called only once per frame in a deferred process.

constants.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_PHYSICS
constants.summary: Set a flag to process modification during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).

constants.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_IDLE
constants.summary: Set a flag to process modification during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).

constants.name: MODIFIER_CALLBACK_MODE_PROCESS_MANUAL
constants.summary: Do not process modification. Use [method advance] to process the modification manually.
